risk_rationale: The introduction of a harmonised age verification method by the European Commission presents a medium risk as it requires adaptation to new regulatory standards but does not pose an immediate existential threat or high material compliance gap.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Detection and prevention using machine learning and safety review to identify likely under-13 users.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Blueprint for age verification and interoperability with EU Digital Identity Wallets.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline relies on machine learning and safety reviews, which may not align with the new EU harmonised age verification method and its interoperability requirements.

remediations.title: Implement EU Harmonised Age Verification
remediations.description: Develop and integrate a system that complies with the EU's harmonised age verification blueprint, ensuring interoperability with EU Digital Identity Wallets.
acceptance_criteria: Age verification system is compliant with EU harmonised standards.
System interoperates with EU Digital Identity Wallets.
Successful testing and validation with EU regulatory bodies.

business_impact: Adapting to the EU's harmonised age verification method may require significant development resources and could affect user onboarding processes in the EU market.
recommended_actions: Initiate a project to assess the technical requirements for compliance with the EU harmonised age verification method.
Engage with EU regulatory bodies to ensure alignment with the new standards.
Develop a timeline and resource allocation plan for implementing the necessary changes.
